summary:
The provided materials contain no discussion of protein initiation codes or the sequence of newly formed peptides; thus, claiming all proteins start with methionine has zero support within these sources. Even though biologically accurate, such claims are invalid under strict interpretation requiring empirical backing from the presented data.

---

Final decision after reevaluation using only stated facts:  
No—because none of the cited texts address how proteins originate structurally via transcription/translation processes involving specific starter residues like methionine.

Therefore,

$\boxed{\text{B}}$
